captions.youtube:
Describe a task and Claude reads your code, proposes a diff you approve, then runs the tests. Read, edit, verify -- you stay in the loop on every change.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding
captions.linkedin:
Claude Code works as an agentic loop: you describe a task, it reads the relevant code and proposes a diff, you approve, then it runs the tests.

The approval step is the point. You review every change before it lands, and verification is part of the loop -- not an afterthought.

Read, edit, verify, repeat.

#ClaudeCode #DeveloperTools #AI
captions.x:
The Claude Code agentic loop: describe a task, it reads your code and proposes a diff, you approve, it runs the tests.

Read, edit, verify. You approve every change before it lands.

#ClaudeCode #AICoding

captions.youtube:
Stop re-explaining your project to Claude Code every session. CLAUDE.md is one file your agent reads automatically on every run. Use /init to draft it from your codebase, then refine.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding
captions.linkedin:
CLAUDE.md is a single file your agent reads at the start of every session, so it already knows your stack, conventions, and structure.

Run /init to generate a first draft straight from your codebase, then trim it to what matters. Less context-setting each session means more consistent output and fewer repeated corrections.

#ClaudeCode #AI #SoftwareDevelopment

captions.youtube:
Claude writes out its plan first. You read it, edit it, approve it. Only then does it touch a file. No surprise edits.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding
captions.linkedin:
Plan mode in Claude Code shows the full plan before it edits anything. You review the steps, adjust them, then approve.

It matters because you stay the reviewer instead of cleaning up after surprise changes. You catch a wrong assumption at the plan stage, not in the diff.

#ClaudeCode #AICoding #DeveloperTools

captions.linkedin:
Claude Code ships with built-in slash commands, and you can add your own by saving them in .claude/commands/.

Commit that folder to git and every teammate gets the same commands. Shared prompts become part of the repo, not tribal knowledge in someone's notes.

captions.youtube:
Made a bad edit? Rewind your code and the conversation back to any earlier point with /rewind or a quick Esc-Esc.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding
captions.linkedin:
Claude Code can undo more than your last change. With /rewind (or Esc-Esc) you can roll both your code and the conversation back to any earlier checkpoint.

That means you can explore a risky change freely and step back cleanly when it doesn't work out, without manually reverting files.

#ClaudeCode #DeveloperTools #AICoding

captions.youtube:
Spin up subagents in Claude Code so each gets its own context window and tools, then run separate tasks in parallel instead of one at a time.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding
captions.linkedin:
Claude Code can run parallel subagents, each with its own context window and tool access.

That means independent tasks (refactor here, write tests there, investigate a bug) move at the same time instead of queuing behind one another, and a long-running task never pollutes your main context.

#ClaudeCode #AI #DeveloperTools
captions.x:
Claude Code subagents each get their own context window and tools, so you can run separate tasks in parallel instead of one at a time. Main context stays clean while they work.

#ClaudeCode #AICoding

captions.youtube:
A PostToolUse hook runs your formatter or linter automatically on every file Claude edits, so changes land already formatted.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding
captions.linkedin:
Claude Code hooks fire on lifecycle events. A PostToolUse hook runs after each file edit, so you can trigger your formatter or linter automatically on every file Claude touches.

It keeps the agent's output consistent with your project standards without you having to ask, and without a manual cleanup pass before commit.

#ClaudeCode #DeveloperTools #AI
captions.x:
A PostToolUse hook in Claude Code runs after every file edit. Wire it to your formatter or linter and Claude's changes land already formatted, no manual cleanup.

#ClaudeCode #DevTools
captions.tiktok:
stop reformatting after the agent, let a hook do it

captions.youtube:
Add one MCP server config and Claude Code can hit your database, call your APIs, or drive the browser directly. No glue scripts, no copy-paste.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding #MCP
captions.linkedin:
MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets Claude Code talk to live systems. Add one server config and Claude can query your database, call your APIs, or control the browser.

It turns the model from a code generator into something that can act against your real tools, with the access scope you define.

#ClaudeCode #MCP #DeveloperTools
captions.x:
One MCP server config and Claude Code gets live tools: query your database, call your APIs, drive the browser. No glue code in between.

captions.linkedin:

captions.youtube:
Long Claude Code sessions get slow and pricey. Run /context to see what's filling the window, /compact to summarize history, /clear to reset between tasks.
Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.
#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding
captions.linkedin:
Long Claude Code sessions accumulate context until responses slow down and cost climbs.

Three commands keep a session lean: /context shows what is taking up the window, /compact summarizes the history so far, and /clear resets it when you switch tasks. Reaching for the right one keeps the model focused on what actually matters.

#ClaudeCode #AI #DeveloperTools
captions.x: Keep long Claude Code sessions lean: /context to inspect what's filling the window, /compact to summarize history, /clear to reset between tasks. #ClaudeCode #AICoding
captions.tiktok:
stop letting your claude code session bloat
#claudecode #ai #coding #developer #tech #softwareengineer #vibecoding

captions.youtube:
Closed your terminal? Pick up the exact conversation with claude --continue for the last session, or claude --resume to choose from a list. /resume switches sessions mid-flight.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding
captions.linkedin:
Claude Code keeps your session history. Run claude --continue to resume the last conversation, or claude --resume to pick a past session from a list. Switch sessions mid-task with /resume.

This matters because context is expensive to rebuild. Resuming keeps the full thread so you don't re-explain the codebase after every break.

#ClaudeCode #DeveloperTools #AI
captions.x:
Closed Claude Code mid-task?

claude --continue  → resume the last session
claude --resume    → pick from past sessions
/resume            → switch sessions in-flight

Full context, no re-explaining.

#ClaudeCode #AICoding
captions.tiktok:
never re-explain your codebase again, just resume the session

#claudecode #ai #coding #developer #tech #softwareengineer #vibecoding

captions.youtube:
Pipe any text into claude -p and get a one-shot answer back, no interactive session. Perfect for scripts, git hooks, and CI.

Follow @RuntheDocs for a daily Claude Code tip.

#ClaudeCode #AI #Coding #DevTools #AICoding #CLI
captions.linkedin:
Claude Code has a headless mode: pipe input into `claude -p "..."` and it returns a single answer, then exits.

That one flag turns Claude into a building block you can drop into shell scripts, git hooks, and CI pipelines instead of a tool you sit in front of.

#ClaudeCode #DeveloperTools #Automation
captions.x:
Headless mode in Claude Code:

cat error.log | claude -p "summarize the root cause"

One-shot answer, no interactive session. Pipe it into scripts, hooks, and CI.

#ClaudeCode #DevTools

Run /sandbox, pick auto-allow, and Claude runs shell commands inside an OS-enforced box with no approval prompts. Research preview.

captions.linkedin:
Claude Code's /sandbox runs shell commands inside an OS-enforced sandbox. Turn on auto-allow and the approval prompts stop, while the OS still contains what the commands can touch.

Fewer interruptions on routine work, with isolation handled at the system level instead of one yes/no at a time. Currently a research preview.
